source.name: Chrome DevTools Remote Debugging
source.license_notes: Summarized guidance with source link; do not copy full docs.

goal: Connect automation to an existing logged-in Chromium browser through a local remote debugging port.
when_to_use: MFA makes fresh browser login hard
steps: Start Chrome/Edge with remote debugging bound to localhost and a dedicated user-data-dir/profile.
Connect Playwright or CDP client to the browser WebSocket endpoint.
Reuse logged-in state but verify account/profile before acting.
Close debug browser or port when done.
safety_notes: Do not expose CDP port to network.
handoff_report: Any irreversible action skipped or confirmed

quality.notes: Chrome remote debugging exposes CDP endpoints; local security rules added.

retrieval.ai_summary: Use CDP when an agent must control an already logged-in Chrome/Edge session: launch with localhost remote-debugging port, verify /json/version, connect, confirm account, and close the port after use.
